title: secamone elliptica subsp. minutiflora
intro: Secamone elliptica subsp. minutiflora is a rare plant species belonging to the Apocynaceae family, also known as the dogbane or oleander family. Traditionally, this plant has been used in folk medicine, and it is reported to possess medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial activities. The health benefits associated with Secamone elliptica subsp. minutiflora include its potential to treat various ailments such as fever, rheumatism, and skin infections. Herbal preparations of this plant are often made by infusing the leaves or stems in hot water to create teas or decoctions, which can be used for medicinal purposes.
